author: DHayesTrades
content: Hey guys - I'm fairly new here been tradig in the community for  afew weeks now. An issue I keep running into is the stop losses. They seem to be fairly discretionary. Today was the perfect example on NVDA. I entered when I saw clear buyers stepping in on the Wednesday Highs level retest - it then reverses and had two candles testing it. Both candles broke that level (where my stop loss was) but both candles did not close underneath. I stopped out when it broke. The trade then runs amazingly without me lol. Cand someone please inform me how to successfully place and respect the stop losses around these key levels especially during a candle trying to break it?

author: JP400
content:
Mess up today. Took AMD today PMH at 10:01 with 2 Cons. Looking for 2:1. Stop at PMH. Did not get the 2:1. Got scared when it came back and sold 1 Con to minimize loss. Then it stepped up again, so I bought another. Sold 1 Con at the close of 10:05. Pushed again. Held for 2:1. Did not get it. Then crossed my fingers (that well known strategy). Then sold last Con at 10:13 with QQQ selling too.
Tried AMD again at 10:14... (with QQQ above PDH). Lost again.
Tried AMD again at 10:22... (with QQQ above PDH). Lost again.
Then recovered some on 2 QQQ Puts off PML at 10:57. Caught that drop. Nice. And recovered my losses. Green day.
But I didn't stop trading !
I bought 2 QQQ again at 11:05 for a drop to PDL.  But it reversed, and I paid the price on the fast price changes on Friday Puts. Wiped out more gains.
And then... Took 2 more QQQ trades (2 Cons each time) to try to recover. Clawed some back. But just reinforcing terrible habits !
Overall loss on the day.

Sigh... Too tired after a busy day repairing cars yesterday. Wanted to make some money. Forgot the No.1 rule that it's not about making money. Broke my 11am rule, and did not stop at 11am. And also took waaaaaay too many trades.
Sigh...

author: JP400
content: Oh darn it ! I just noticed that I stopped out of my emotional NVDA trade as it dropped below the 5mOR at 10:32.. I also lost out on a great recovery for the day. But I guess the profit would have arguably cemented bad habits of trying to claw back when I should really be quitting for the day. Grrrrrrrr....

author: Moe
content: Daily wicks seem to me to lead to alot of chop and good avoid zones. Look at NVDA and TSLA both we though looked decent for a short but both hit a daily wick zone and chopped.

author: JP400
content: It should be that easy... right ? It's all well and good figuring out which delta to buy and what DTE, and all that stuff. But SPY can move fast and one just doesn't Sell quick enough. Or... one gets in a bit too late into INTC, and then holds for a little too long. So those $50 Risks get a bit bigger than intended. The numbers worked... the execution... not so much.

author: Jdub
content:
Biggest Day Of The Year For Me!!

+12K Realized Intraday Plus Some Trailers Still
+17K Unrealized In Long Term 🙂

Beautiful trade today on TSLA. Hopefully some of you guys were able to capture it. I try my very best to outline the moves before hand in the pre market and in the live sessions. The reason I do them is for you guys to build conviction yourself. There is no better feeling then having your own conviction in a trade. That's why we always preach understanding whats going on instead of relying on "signals". You can follow signals all day but in the end you won't become profitable if don't learn to trade for yourself.

With that being said I just took some time to reflect today and I wanted to let you guys know that trading is hard. We might make it seem easier then it is at times but the reality is that trading is one of the most difficult professions in the world. 

I started trading in 2019. At the time I was living with my grandparents in an unfinished basement. I was in school, was working at Amazon while trying to make trading work. For the first two years I was losing every-single day and it felt like I couldn't even put on a winning trade. Nothing felt like it was going my way. I was struggling everyday but I knew that it was only a matter of time. 

I know some of you guys are in that position right now and I just want to let you guys know that it is going to get better. Everything that you need to succeed in the markets is all in front of you. All you have to do is put in the work. Show up to the pre markets preps, watch the live sessions over and over again, watch the trade reviews. We can layout the full blue print but in the end it is up to YOU to put in the work. I can't come and click the buttons for you. 

Just wanted to end it off here. I usually don't get too emotional or like typing out these motivational speeches but I thought if it can help someone or motivate someone then it would be worth it.  At the end of the day trading is very lonely and I think we've built a great community here and all want each other to win. 

Let's keep killing it team! @everyone

Gameplan July 3rd 

Half day today in the markets. We close at 1:00PM EST.  With that being said the ES hit ATH's in the pre market. That was our last level for the major indices. 

What I would like to see coming into today is the reaction that we have off that level.

As for my top watches today its going to be NVDA and AAPL. 

NVDA looks good above the 157.5 PDH for a push towards the 158.5.

As for AAPL I would like to see it maintain above Tuesdays highs.
